title: Choose GKE Autopilot or Standard by operational ownership
description: Pick Autopilot when you want Google to manage nodes and pay per-pod, Standard when you need node-level control, then set requests so scheduling works.
body: Use when provisioning GKE or debugging scheduling. Do not run Standard with hand-managed node pools if you only need pods scheduled, and do not expect Autopilot to allow privileged daemonsets or arbitrary node tweaks. Actions: choose Autopilot for hands-off, per-pod-billed clusters and Standard when you need GPUs, specific machine types, or node customization; always set realistic resource requests since Autopilot bills and schedules on them; enable Workload Identity. Validation signals include pods scheduling without Insufficient cpu/memory events and Autopilot provisioning capacity automatically. Risk boundary: switching a production cluster's mode or node pool is human-gated.
